have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun (z : ℂ) => (z : ℂ) - 1 : ℂ → ℂ) (Set.Ioi (0 : ℝ)) := by
    apply ContDiffOn.sub
    · exact contDiffOn_id
    · exact contDiffOn_const
  have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun (z : ℂ) => (z : ℂ) - 1 : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := by
    apply h₁.mono
    <;> intro z hz
    <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, upperHalfPlaneSet]
    <;> norm_num at *
    <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try linarith) <;>
    (try nlinarith)
  have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun (z : ℂ) => 1 / (z - 1 : ℂ) : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := by
    apply ContDiffOn.div
    · exact contDiffOn_const
    · exact h₂
    · intro z hz
      simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, upperHalfPlaneSet]
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try linarith) <;>
      (try nlinarith)
      <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, Complex.normSq, pow_two]
          <;>
          nlinarith
        })
  have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun (z : ℂ) => (1 : ℝ) / (z - 1 : ℂ) : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := by
    simpa using h₃
  have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun (z : ℂ) => Real.exp (-(r : ℝ) * Complex.abs (z - 1) ^ 2 : ℝ) : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := by
    apply ContDiffOn.exp
    apply ContDiffOn.neg
    apply ContDiffOn.mul
    · exact contDiffOn_const
    · apply ContDiffOn.pow
      apply ContDiffOn.abs
      apply ContDiffOn.sub
      · exact contDiffOn_id
      · exact contDiffOn_const
  have h₆ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₄' r) ℍ₀ := by
    apply ContDiffOn.mul
    · apply h₅
    · apply h₃
  exact h₆

have h : Φ₄' r = Φ₃' r := by
    funext z
    simp [Φ₄', Φ₃']
    <;> ring_nf
    <;> field_simp
    <;> ring_nf
  rw [h]
  have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := by
    have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℂ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := Φ₃'_contDiffOn_ℂ
    have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Use the fact that complex smoothness implies real smoothness
      have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (Φ₃' r : ℂ → ℂ) z) ℍ₀ := by
        -- Convert complex smoothness to real smoothness
        have h₆ : ContDiffOn ℂ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := h₃
        -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
        have h₇ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (Φ₃' r : ℂ → ℂ) z : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := by
          apply (contDiffOn_iff_contDiffOn_real.ext_iff.2 fun x hx => by
            -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
            have h₈ : ContDiffAt ℂ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (Φ₃' r : ℂ → ℂ) z) x := by
              apply h₆.contDiffAt
              exact hx
            have h₉ : ContDiffAt ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (Φ₃' r : ℂ → ℂ) z : ℂ → ℂ) x := by
              -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
              apply Complex.contDiffAt_of_contDiffAtComplex h₈
            exact h₉).symm
        exact h₇
      -- Use the fact that the function is complex smooth to conclude real smoothness
      have h₈ : (fun z : ℂ => (Φ₃' r : ℂ → ℂ) z) = (Φ₃' r : ℂ → ℂ) := rfl
      have h₉ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := by
        simpa [h₈] using h₅
      exact h₉
    exact h₄
  exact h₂

    have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := by

    have h₂ : (Φ₄' r : ℂ → ℂ) = (Φ₃' r : ℂ → ℂ) := by

have h_Φ₃'_real_smooth : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := by
    have h₁ : ContDiffOn ℂ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := Φ₃'_contDiffOn_ℂ
    -- Convert the complex ContDiffOn statement to a real ContDiffOn statement
    have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
      have h₃ : ∀ n : ℕ, ContDiffOn ℝ n (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := by
        intro n
        -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
        have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ n (Φ₃' r) ℍ₀ := by
          -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
          refine' (h₁.contDiffOn_real_of_contDiffOn_complex _).trans _
          -- The set ℍ₀ is open in ℂ and hence in ℝ²
          <;> simp_all [isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet]
          <;> norm_num
          <;>
          (try
            {
              -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
              exact inferInstance
            })
          <;>
          (try
            {
              -- Use the fact that complex differentiability implies real differentiability
              exact inferInstance
            })
        exact h₄
      -- Since this holds for all n, it holds for ∞
      exact contDiffOn_of_forall_contDiffOn h₃
    exact h₂
  
  have h_Φ₄'_eq_Φ₃' : Φ₄' r = Φ₃' r := by
    simp [Φ₄', Φ₃']
    <;>
    (try ring_nf) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]) <;>
    (try field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try ring_nf) <;>
    (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]) <;>
    (try field_simp [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two]) <;>
    (try norm_num)
    <;>
    aesop
  
  have h_main : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₄' r) ℍ₀ := by
    rw [h_Φ₄'_eq_Φ₃']
    exact h_Φ₃'_real_smooth
  
  exact h_main

have h₁ : φ₀''_holo := by
    -- Prove that φ₀'' is holomorphic on the upper half-plane
    have h : Holo(φ₀'') := by
      -- Use the fact that φ₀'' is a polynomial in z and its conjugate
      -- and hence is holomorphic.
      apply DifferentiableOn.mul
      · -- The real part is holomorphic
        apply DifferentiableOn.pow
        · apply DifferentiableOn.sub
          · apply DifferentiableOn.add
            · exact differentiableOn_id
            · exact (differentiableOn_const _)
          · exact (differentiableOn_const _)
        · -- The power is holomorphic
          exact (by norm_num)
      · -- The imaginary part is holomorphic
        apply DifferentiableOn.pow
        · apply DifferentiableOn.sub
          · apply DifferentiableOn.add
            · exact differentiableOn_id
            · exact (differentiableOn_const _)
          · exact (differentiableOn_const _)
        · -- The power is holomorphic
          exact (by norm_num)
    exact h
  
  have h₂ : Φ₄'_holo := by
    -- Prove that Φ₄' r is holomorphic on the upper half-plane
    have h : Holo(Φ₄' r) := by
      -- Use the fact that Φ₄' r is a product of holomorphic functions
      -- and hence is holomorphic.
      have h₃ : Holo(Φ₄' r) := Φ₄'_holo
      exact h₃
    exact h
  
  -- Use the fact that Φ₄' r is holomorphic on the upper half-plane
  -- to conclude that it is smooth on the upper half-plane.
  have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₄' r) ℍ₀ := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₄' r is holomorphic on the upper half-plane
    -- to conclude that it is smooth on the upper half-plane.
    have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₄' r) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Use the fact that Φ₄' r is holomorphic on the upper half-plane
      -- to conclude that it is smooth on the upper half-plane.

    exact h₄
  
  exact h₃

    funext z
    <;> rfl
  rw [h]
  exact Φ₃'_contDiffOn

have : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₄' r) ℍ₀ := Φ₃'_contDiffOn
  exact this

have h : Φ₄' r = Φ₃' r := rfl
  rw [h]
  -- Use the fact that Φ₃' is already known to be smooth on the upper half-plane.
  exact Φ₃'_contDiffOn
